mapping.a_element: an r-minimal P-code (the extremal combinatorial object)
mapping.b_element: an optimal screening mechanism / bundle menu

mapping.a_element: cutting r-blocking set/map — a configuration that cuts (covers) every relevant hyperplane/subspace
mapping.b_element: the 'covered' directions generated by complementarity among bundles

mapping.a_element: the family of hyperplanes/subspaces that must be cut
mapping.b_element: the 'required' directions of binding feasibility constraints fixed by the type distribution

mapping.a_element: r-minimality holds iff the cutting r-blocking (full-coverage) condition is met
mapping.b_element: optimality holds iff the required directions are fully covered

mapping.a_element: generalized Ashikhmin-Barg / (P,ω)-weight sufficient criterion for minimality
mapping.b_element: threshold plus inclusivity distributional condition guaranteeing the optimal menu

brief_justification: Both papers use a duality to reduce an extremality question to one geometric certificate: the covering/blocking set must include the entire required family of directions/hyperplanes. A code is r-minimal exactly when its cutting r-blocking set cuts every hyperplane, mirroring the result that a mechanism is optimal exactly when the complementarity-covered directions cover all required binding-constraint directions; minimality<->optimality and blocking-set<->covered-directions map role-for-role, with the (P,ω)-weight/Ashikhmin-Barg criterion and the inclusivity threshold serving as the matching sufficient conditions.
